instructions: Choose the best answer for each question. These questions test your ability to visualise shapes, patterns, and spatial relationships. You have 15 minutes to complete this section.

items.text: A square piece of paper is folded in half once, then a corner is cut off. How many holes appear when the paper is unfolded?
If you rotate the letter 'L' 90 degrees clockwise, which shape do you get?
How many faces does a triangular prism have?
Which shape is the mirror image of a 'P' reflected along a vertical axis?
A cube has each face painted a different colour: Red (top), Blue (bottom), Green (front), Yellow (back), White (left), Black (right). If you tilt the cube forward 90°, which colour is now on top?
A flat cross-shaped net (one centre square and four surrounding squares) is folded into a box. Which face is opposite the centre square?
options: The box has no opposite face — it is open-topped
The centre square is opposite itself

items.text: Count the total number of squares (of all sizes) in a 3×3 grid.
A shape is made of 5 unit cubes: 3 in a row, plus 1 on top of the left cube and 1 on top of the right cube. How many unit cubes would be needed to fill this into a complete 3×2×1 rectangular block?
Which of these 2D shapes CANNOT tile a flat surface without gaps or overlaps using only copies of itself?
You are facing North. You turn 90° clockwise, then 180°, then 90° anti-clockwise. Which direction are you now facing?
A solid cylinder is cut with a plane at a 45° angle to its base. What shape is the cross-section?
A 4×4×4 cube is built from 64 unit cubes. The entire outer surface is painted. How many unit cubes have NO painted face?
A piece of paper is folded in half twice (into quarters), then a single circular hole is punched through all layers in the centre. How many holes are visible when the paper is fully unfolded?
Two gears are meshed. Gear A has 20 teeth and Gear B has 30 teeth. If Gear A rotates 3 full turns clockwise, how many turns does Gear B make and in which direction?
A wireframe cube is viewed from directly above one corner (so you look straight down the space diagonal). What 2D shape do you see?
